rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Ma'S Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp Sec 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 6, 2020, line 2. DEFENDANT CHARLES MA'S Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp Sec 128.7 Hearing Required. The Court is inclined to deny the motion as factual determinations necessary to determine propriety of imposition of sanctions under CCP 128.7 as to the Plaintiff alone would infringe on attorney-client relationship. The Court notes that the complaint was filed by a represented party, this motion was served when the party was still represented, the safe harbor expired while the party was still represented, motion to withdraw was filed 7 days later. While a represented party is subject to sanction under CCP 128.7(b)(3), the Court is not inclined to disturb the sanctity of attorney-client relationship under the circumstances of this case. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 6, 2020, line 2. DEFENDANT CHARLES MA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Hearing Required. By no later than 8:30 a.m. on August 6, 2020 Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief limited to responses to the following: (1) the date the contract between Plaintiff and Ma was entered into; (2) the total amount Ma was to pay to Plaintiff under the contract; (3) if the payment was to be made in installments, what were the dates and amounts of the payments; (4) what was the move-out date; (4) list of any additional terms of the contract, e.g. neutral recommendation to prospective landlords, execution of the agreement in exchange for any part of payment, etc.; (5) if the contract was signed by both parties, attach a copy of the contract as Exhibit A; (6) if the contract was not signed by either party, attach (a) any writing evidencing Plaintiff's acceptance of each of the terms described in sections 2,3 and 4 as Exhibit B and (b) any writing evidencing Ma's acceptance of each of the terms described in sextions 2, 3, and 4; (7) if the contract was oral or implied, provide specific citations to the evidence submitted in opposition to the motion on July 13, 2020 as to the formation of the contract and terms of the contract as stated in sections 1, 2, 3 and 4. The format of the supplemental brief shall be as follows: Restate the question and provide facts and/or citations to evidence without any argument. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DION ROBERTS VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING LLC A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 6, 2020, line 3 . DEFENDANTS PHH MORTGAGE CORPORATION, WESTERN PROGRESSIVE, LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SOMONA CO.** JANE DOE ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue And Award For Reasonable Expenses And Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 6, 2020, line 4. DEFENDANTS STANLEY ANDERSON, CATHRYN ANDERSON'S Motion To Change Venue And Award For Reasonable Expenses And Fees Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Change Venue. It appears that the parties agreed to the change of venue as evidenced by the stipulation submitted on July 31, 2020. Stipulation is silent as to the payment of costs. Hearing Required to address the payment of costs. If parties agree regarding the costs prior to the hearing, parties may file an amended stipulation and order to avoid a hearing by 8:30 a.m. on August 6, 2020. A copy of the amended stipulation and order, if any, to be provided to Dept501Orders@sftc.org. = (501/CFH)
